dayName: First Day at the Crow's Wing

scenarios.title: The Merchant's Lost Wagon
scenarios.target: recover the salt-merchant's wagon from the brigands camped at the Crow's Ford

contributions.line: Marek's brawny hands strain against the strongbox lock, feeling the cold metal give just enough.
Roselle slips through the treeline, her quick steps avoiding every snapped twig and rustle.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Despite their combined efforts, the lock holds firm, and Roselle's cautious approach fails to bypass the sentry unnoticed, leaving the wagon unclaimed.

scenarios.title: The Brigand's Tongue
scenarios.target: wring the location of the bandit cache out of the prisoner before dawn

contributions.line: Marek's muscular frame presses into the prisoner, his brawny arm ready to crush falsehoods.
Veska watches the prisoner’s eyes for contradictions, her sharp mind catching the smallest shifts in his story.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek’s physical intimidation and Veska’s keen eye expose enough lies to break the prisoner’s resolve. Under the strain, he finally reveals the rough location of the bandit's cache, just before dawn.

scenarios.title: Watchtower at the Pass
scenarios.target: commission a new watchtower at the southern pass before winter closes the road

contributions.line: Veska's precise calculations ensure the foundation will withstand the spring melt, despite her fatigue dulling her usual sharpness.
Marek keeps the crew in line with a brawny glare, but his fatigue makes him less vigilant for the subtle flaws in the timber.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: The watchtower's design was sound on paper, but Marek's oversight faltered as fatigue set in, leading to compromised quality in the construction.
